materialChanges: Corrected the storage-model assessment by separating the T = 73 solution from a T = 74 hypothetical, then aligned the answer, reasoning, common errors, and rubric so substitution and domain admissibility are tested separately.
Specified millilitres, millilitres per minute, and minutes in the transfer task and added the dimensional check showing that Q/r and t0 are both times.
Mapped claim-unit-independent-rearrangement to scene-orientation and added it to that scene's learner-visible source note.
Rebuilt the starting diagnostic as an attempt-first check with arithmetic-estimation and signed-arithmetic prerequisite questions, complete feedback, and recovery guidance.
Placed the worked-example retrieval response, grouped practice prompts and feedback, and independent transfer prompt and solution inside check directives with prompts before feedback.
Removed meta-level acceptable-response descriptions from rubric-scored items and retained only a literal algebraically equivalent response string where appropriate.
Removed a learner-facing internal filename, clarified agent-assistance attribution for the review/finalization sequence, and refreshed the NIST source-access and rights check date.

reviewDispositions.rationale: The transfer-counter prompt now gives n = 22.4 only in a separate T = 74 case. The key verifies 2.5(22.4) + 18 = 74 and then rejects 22.4 solely because the batch-count domain requires a nonnegative integer; reasoning, errors, and rubric were updated consistently.
The transfer scene now declares Q in mL, r in mL/min, and t and t0 in min, and explicitly simplifies Q/r to minutes before adding it to t0.
scene-orientation was added to claim-unit-independent-rearrangement.sceneIds and the orientation source note now names that claim with source-nist-si-chapter7.

The finding confirms the NIST-backed quantity-equation and numerical-value-equation claims are accurate and bounded. Finalizer inspection of NIST sections 7.1 and 7.11 found no corrective content change necessary.
Independent recalculation confirmed the worked example still reduces to x = 6 and substitutes to 49 on both sides, so the positive finding requires no content change.
The Starting check is now a check directive containing five attempt-first questions followed by answers, explanations, and specific recovery routes.
All four practice prompts now precede a grouped feedback section inside check-faded-practice, preserving an attempt-before-feedback route and keeping diagnostic next steps in the recovery section.
The transfer prompt and its post-attempt worked feedback are now contained in check-transfer-rate-time, with the independent task presented before any solution steps.

The rubric-scored fraction and transfer items now use empty acceptableResponses arrays, while the formula item retains only the literal equivalent response h = (2A - bk)/b, b ≠ 0. Every retained entry is a JSON string.
The starting diagnostic now checks the declared arithmetic-estimation prerequisite with a rounded quotient and a signed-magnitude estimate, then supplies targeted recovery guidance.
The explanation for check-original-equation was moved inside that check directive and explicitly labelled as feedback after the learner's attempt.

The finding positively evaluates the travelling-term misconception repair; finalizer inspection found its elicitation, inverse-operation explanation, and formula-rearrangement extension accurate and coherent.

The finding confirms the candidate identity, structure, objectives, and file mappings. The final revision preserves lesson ID PREM-QNT-004, version 0.1.0, draft status, scene order, and objective mappings.

evidenceChecked: All seven scene files have one level-one heading, logical subordinate headings, and an ordered text-first reading path matching lesson.json; each directive is from the documented declarative whitelist and all directive fences are balanced.

Prompts precede feedback in the diagnostic, worked-example retrieval, practice, and transfer checks. The canonical source preserves every prompt, solution, recovery route, and assessment condition without requiring color, position, sound, motion, hover, dragging, timing, or pointer-only action.

The lesson contains no figures, charts, audio, video, datasets, or third-party assets. Important equations are accompanied by prose explanations, substitution checks, domain statements, and, in the transfer scene, explicit unit relationships.

Static security inspection found no raw HTML, JavaScript, remote embed, Markdown image, executable code fence, unsafe URL scheme, symlink, secret, credential, personal data, patient material, clinical direction, or unsafe experiment in the pack.

Every scene is claims-mapped and each mapped claim appears in a same-scene learner-visible source note resolving to source-nist-si-chapter7; claims retain bounded scope, uncertainty, routine risk, and pending-review status.

The NIST Chapter 7 source page was checked for the quantity-equation and numerical-value-equation distinction and its current version date; the NIST copyrights/disclaimers page was checked for public-information reuse terms and attribution expectations.

references.json records an allowed official-standard type, exact URL/version/locator, facts-only use, original-expression rights basis, dated access status, and concise bounded fields. ATTRIBUTION.md records CC BY 4.0, accountable authorship, original synthesis, fictional examples, no reused media, and the review/finalizer provenance route.
limitations: This was a static source audit; no rendered browser, keyboard-only, screen-reader, 200% zoom/reflow, reduced-motion, print, or unavailable non-web export test was performed.

Per the operator's explicit scope, no file under site/ was read, so the public schemas and quorum JSON were not independently inspected in this run; artifact shape follows the supplied policy/schema notes and the permitted adjudication template.

Per the operator's explicit instruction, npm validation and strict source preflight were not run. JSON parsing, internal identity, duration, point totals, string-type, field-length, directive, heading, claim-note, and static security checks passed.
The operator supplied that both review artifacts have equivalent non-dismissed COMMENTED transports on pull request 60; this run did not independently query GitHub.
No real learner testing or assistive-technology user testing was available.

rationale: The standard-lesson input gate is satisfied on the committed artifacts and the operator-supplied GitHub transport facts: exactly one academic and one learning-design request-changes input target the original candidate, use distinct run IDs and declared providers/model families, and are eligible advisory records. No proposed review was excluded. All thirteen indexed findings are disposed. The final revision corrects the only factual assessment error, closes the unit and claim-traceability gaps, restores prerequisite diagnosis and attempt-before-feedback structure, and repairs acceptable-response semantics without changing lesson identity, version, status, outcome scope, or the review artifacts. Recalculation and static internal checks found the worked and assessed algebra consistent. The final accessibility-and-rights audit found a semantic text-first pack with no sensory-only dependency, active content, personal data, third-party asset, or incompatible reuse; the single NIST source supports the bounded claims and is used facts-only in original expression. Both advisory inputs dissented from the original candidate through request-changes verdicts, and their material concerns were incorporated. Merge is therefore warranted subject to final-commit stamping and the repository/transport checks explicitly reserved to the operator.
dissent: The academic reviewer requested changes for a false n = 22.4 assessment statement, unspecified transfer units, and an omitted orientation claim mapping; all three requested repairs were incorporated, while its two affirmative notes required no change.

The learning-design reviewer requested changes for missing diagnostic feedback, exposed practice/transfer answers, meta-level acceptable responses, missing prerequisite estimation checks, and an exposed worked-check answer; all six requested repairs were incorporated, while its two affirmative notes required no change.
conditions: The operator must commit the final content and both cited review artifacts, replace PENDING-FINAL-COMMIT with that exact 40-character SHA, and make no teaching-content change after the recorded final commit.
Before merge, the operator must run the required strict source preflight and repository validation and resolve any failure without misrepresenting this artifact as having run npm checks.
The protected check must confirm that both operator-supplied COMMENTED GitHub review transports remain equivalent and non-dismissed.
Any publication transition remains maintainer-only and is limited to lesson status, sourceConfidence, and retained claim reviewStatus fields permitted after finalization.

adjudicator.displayName: VSBDev — accountable operator and fresh finalizer
